what_it_does: Implements a single generic runtime renderer, DNA.renderArchetype, that turns an archetype layout record plus a data record into a rendered decision card DOM element. It also provides DNA.renderExplained for composing a contained interface mockup alongside explanatory slide content, including binary and N-option comparison views.
observations: The file is wrapped in an IIFE that attaches DNA to window.
Slot values are resolved from a flat record using a slot_map, dotted paths, or live 'resolve:<name>' sources.
Options render as full tiles for ≤2 choices and ledger rows for 3+ choices, with a position-driven metal colour ramp and gold reserved for the recommended option.
Full-card 'empty' and 'failed' states replace the body zone with a centered state moment.
Composed explained views generate their own binary or N-option comparison panels with inline SVG visuals and wire panel CTAs to window.galleryBinder.decide.
Live DNA theme resolution is applied via DNA.injectVars/DNA.injectSpace, with a cached fetch fallback for tokens/grammar JSON.
Inline onclick handlers are generated for options, skip/retry buttons, and comparison-panel CTAs.
standouts: A self-contained inline VI logo SVG is embedded so the V-mark renders without depending on unit-view.js.
The renderer distinguishes telegraphic, medium, and long hero question copy via length-aware CSS classes.
Binary decisions get a custom two-panel comparison with generated SVG scenes rather than the standard card options.
Async 'resolve:' slot values stream into the DOM and show a streaming cursor class while pending.
conventions: Uses a global DNA namespace and an esc utility for HTML escaping.
CSS class names follow a design-system vocabulary (.p-*, .dc-*, .ar-*).

Addresses are expected to be frame-explicit (decision://global/ or decision://project/

/) and a warning is emitted otherwise.
Functional decomposition: small helpers for picking, slot rendering, options, zones, states, and composed views.
concerns: Inline onclick attributes bind to window.galleryBinder and window.dispatchEvent, creating tight coupling to host globals and making event handling harder to trace.
The tightPts and tightPtsN split regex is complex and may mangle implication text unexpectedly.
Comparison-panel CTAs use setAttribute('onclick', ...) with stringified JSON, which is brittle and a potential XSS vector if label/title values are not properly escaped.
The file mixes rendering, event wiring, async data resolution, and visual generation in one module.
notes: This is the shared runtime renderer that tries to unify gallery, standalone, and live decision surfaces under one generic archetype-driven renderer while also carrying bespoke composed-view logic for binary and N-option decision explanations.
questions: Where are the archetype records in dna/layouts.json defined and how do slot_map entries relate to record fields?
What contract does opts.resolve follow for async 'resolve:<name>' sources, and who provides it?
How does phone.css map the declared slot/zone surfaces to actual visual ground/depth?
What is the expected lifecycle for el._showWriteError and which host calls it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a single generic renderer for any archetype; the file largely does that, but also contains substantial bespoke logic for binary/N-option comparison panels and inline SVG scene generation, so it is more than a pure generic renderer.
apparent_themes: Avoiding prototype-style chrome in real decision cards
intention_signals: The code appears to be trying to collapse several previously separate render paths (gallery specimen renderer, live decision card, standalone piece, composed slide view) into one shared runtime, while preserving the richer explanatory visuals that were previously bespoke.
novelty: The combination of a generic archetype renderer with self-contained composed comparison views that generate their own SVG scene diagrams and wire live takes back through the same galleryBinder mechanism.
contribution: A reusable archetype-to-DOM renderer that centralizes slot resolution, option presentation, full-card state moments, async streaming, and DNA theme application; plus a composed-view generator that can embed any rendered interface alongside explanatory binary/N-option panels.
summary_for_embedding: archetype.js is a shared runtime renderer that maps archetype layout records and data records into live decision-card DOM elements and can compose them inside explanatory slide views with generated comparison panels.
intention_for_embedding: To provide one generic, theme-aware, async-capable renderer for all decision surfaces and their explanatory presentations.

symbols.does: Safely reads a dotted path from an object, returning undefined for missing intermediate keys.
symbols.description: A tiny path getter used by the slot resolver to pull nested values out of the data record using dot-separated keys.

symbols.does: Determines which record field supplies a slot's value.
symbols.description: Returns the slot's mapped source from slot_map, or slot.from, or falls back to the slot id itself, giving the renderer a single source identifier per slot.

symbols.does: Renders one slot's resolved value into design-system HTML based on its accepted type and role.
symbols.description: Maps slot metadata to components such as hero question headings, what-kind labels, voice explanation cards, lists, options, marks, and a shape/visual-device slot; applies length-aware classes for long questions.

symbols.does: Builds the options region for a decision, including state banners, decided moments, and the option list.
symbols.description: Chooses between tile and row layouts depending on option count, renders a gold decided moment when applicable, includes an error banner, and appends a 'Not now' ghost button when skippable.

symbols.does: Renders a single full-tile option button for binary or two-option decisions.
symbols.description: Produces a large tappable button with label, optional recommended/chosen badges, description, implication, and an explicit 'Choose' or 'Chosen' verb to read as a button rather than an info panel.

symbols.does: Renders a single ledger-row option button for decisions with three or more options.
symbols.description: Produces a compact row with an ordinal letter, label, description, implication, check ring, and a position-driven metal colour ramp that reserves gold for the recommended option.

symbols.does: Renders one archetype zone by rendering each of its slots and wrapping them in a zone container.
symbols.description: Walks a zone's slots, suppresses explanation slots in composed mode, skips empty optional slots, and emits surface classes declared on slots or zones.

symbols.does: Renders full-card placeholder HTML for empty or failed decision states.
symbols.description: Returns a centered state moment with a mark, heading, subtext, and retry button for the 'empty' and 'failed' card states.

symbols.does: Fetches a small icon from the DNA.org icon helper, defaulting to an empty string.
symbols.description: A local helper used inside renderExplained to request 16px icons in the current gold colour for inline shape-line and arrow visuals.

symbols.does: Builds an async-capable grounded-explanation voice card for composed views.
symbols.description: Resolves an explanation source, renders a placeholder with a streaming cursor while pending, and returns both the HTML and a wire function that streams the resolved text and optional why/grounding fields into the DOM.

symbols.does: Attaches the async explanation resolution to a rendered root element.
symbols.description: Called after the composed view is inserted; waits for the explanation promise and updates the DOM text, appending why-it-matters and grounding-note sections when present.

symbols.does: Positions an existing SVG organism inside a parent SVG scene at a given location and scale.
symbols.description: Parses the child SVG's viewBox, computes responsive width/height from a target width, and wraps it in a group with an optional drop-shadow filter and rotation.

symbols.does: Composes a shared library scene from three generated SVG artefacts.
symbols.description: Returns a string that places a plan thumbnail, bar chart, and file-stack together with overlap, scale variation, and slight rotation to form a rich design-library cluster.

symbols.does: Draws a small person-shaped actor rectangle inside the comparison SVG scenes.
symbols.description: Renders a rounded rectangle with a head circle and shoulder arc to represent a user or participant in the read/save panel visuals.

symbols.does: Generates an octagon polygon string centered at a given point and radius.
symbols.description: Computes eight vertices rotated by π/8 and returns an SVG polygon element string used as the authorize core shape in the save-back panel visual.

symbols.does: Splits an implication string into a short list of bullet points for binary panels.
symbols.description: Breaks text on sentence separators, trims punctuation, filters short fragments, and limits to three items to fit compact panel point lists.

symbols.does: Renders one side of the binary comparison view.
symbols.description: Builds a panel with a Today/Recommended tag, heading, generated SVG visual, tight implication points, and a CTA button that can be wired to a live take.

symbols.does: Splits an implication string into a short list for N-option comparison panels.
symbols.description: Similar to tightPts but limits to two items per panel to keep multi-option grids compact.

symbols.does: Renders one option panel in the N-option comparison grid.
symbols.description: Produces a panel with an ordinal or Recommended tag, label, optional description, implication points, and a CTA, applying the rec class to highlight the recommended option.

symbols.does: Selects a metal colour class for an option based on position and recommendation.
symbols.description: Returns r-gold for the recommended option and steps remaining options through ochre, bronze, and faint classes by index.

symbols.does: Applies live DNA theme and spacing tokens to a rendered element.
symbols.description: Uses DNA.injectVars and DNA.injectSpace if available; otherwise falls back to cached fetches of tokens.json and grammar.json, applying warmth, polarity, and density to the element.

symbols.does: Applies fetched token and grammar objects to an element via DNA helpers.
symbols.description: A small inner function of resolveOnto that calls injectVars and injectSpace inside try/catch so missing surface helpers fail gracefully.

symbols.does: Resolves a slot's value from the record or from a live resolver.
symbols.description: Uses sourceOf to find the slot source, returns a live resolver value (or '…' for pending promises), and otherwise reads from the flattened record via direct key or dotted path lookup.
